title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region
impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

updates.content:
Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Cloud PCs and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

More info: While impact is specific to Cloud PC connection in the East US region, users in other regions could be affected if they are connecting to Cloud PCs in that region.

Current status: Our service health monitoring alerted us to failures occurring when users attempt to connect to their Cloud PCs located in the East US region. We've identified that a portion of service infrastructure responsible for facilitating user connection to Cloud PCs in the East US is operating in a suboptimal state, causing connection requests to fail and resulting in impact. We're redirecting request traffic to alternate portions of infrastructure while we review recent changes and deployments to determine whether any may be contributing to the impact.

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access their Cloud PCs hosted in the East US may be impacted. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, April 24, 2026, at 11:39 AM UTC

Root cause: A portion of service infrastructure responsible for facilitating user connection to Cloud PCs in the East US is operating in a suboptimal state, causing connection requests to fail and resulting in impact.

Next update time: Friday, April 24, 2026, at 6:00 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

More info: While impact is specific to Cloud PC connection in the East US region, users in other regions could be affected if they are connecting to Cloud PCs in that region.

Current status: We've identified that a recent service change is causing the portion of affected infrastructure to operate in a suboptimal state and have adjusted our root cause statement accordingly. Alongside our efforts to redirect request traffic, we're reversing the offending change to remediate the impact.

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access their Cloud PCs hosted in the East US may be impacted. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

More info: While impact is specific to Cloud PC connection in the East US region, users in other regions could be affected if they are connecting to Cloud PCs in that region.

Current status: Our efforts to redirect request traffic to healthy portions of infrastructure and reverse the aforementioned change is still ongoing. In the meantime, we're monitoring our service health telemetry to confirm that these actions remediate the impact as expected.

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access their Cloud PCs hosted in the East US may be impacted. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, April 24, 2026, at 11:39 AM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

More info: While impact is specific to Cloud PC connection in the East US region, users in other regions could be affected if they are connecting to Cloud PCs in that region.

Current status: Our efforts to redirect request traffic to healthy portions of infrastructure and reverse the aforementioned change have completed. Subsequently, we’re reaching out to a subset of affected users to confirm whether or not additional mitigative actions will be needed. 

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access their Cloud PCs hosted in the East US may be impacted. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, April 24, 2026, at 11:39 AM UTC

Next update time: Saturday, April 25, 2026, at 2:00 AM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to connect to their Windows 365 Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region

User impact: Users may be unable to connect to their Cloud PCs that are hosted in the East US region.

More info: Impact was specific to Cloud PC connection in the East US region, users in other regions could’ve been affected if they were connecting to Cloud PCs in that region.

Final status: After validating with a subset of users and an extended period of monitoring, we’ve validated that our efforts to redirect request traffic to healthy portions of infrastructure and reverse the aforementioned change have remediated impact. 

Scope of impact: Any user who attempted to access their Cloud PCs hosted in the East US may’ve been impacted. 

Start time: Friday, April 24, 2026, at 11:39 AM UTC

End time: Saturday, April 25, 2026, at 12:15 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent service change was causing portion of service infrastructure responsible for facilitating user connection to Cloud PCs in the East US to operate in a suboptimal state. As a result, the connection requests were failing and resulted in impact.


Next steps: 
- We’re continuing to analyze how the service change was applied to understand why the potential for impact wasn't caught during the update testing and validation phase. These actions will help us to clarify what actions we can take to ensure similar impact doesn't occur in future and to continue our ongoing mission to improve the resilience of our service for all Windows 365 users.

This is the final update for the event.
